name: Junie
description: JetBrains' AI coding agent, out of beta since June 2026 and ranked the #1 coding agent on the independent SWE-Rebench benchmark (61.6% resolved, 72.7% pass@5). Junie plans before it codes, debugs with the IDE's real debugger, reviews pull requests with project context, and runs from JetBrains IDEs, a terminal CLI, or CI (GitHub Actions/GitLab). LLM-agnostic with BYOK and local-model support; included in JetBrains AI subscription tiers.

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Ranked #1 coding agent on the latest SWE-Rebench run with 61.6% resolved and 72.7% pass@5, ahead of other agents and competitive with raw frontier models

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Review of independent SWE-Rebench benchmark placement plus GA feature assessment; SWE-Rebench is a continuously refreshed, contamination-resistant benchmark

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Uses the IDE's real toolchain: debugger (breakpoints, stack inspection, expression evaluation), test runners, and DataGrip-configured database connections for SQL validation

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Assessment of IDE-native tool integration (debugger, run configurations, database access) versus shell-only agents

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Plan Mode produces a structured planning document with product requirements, technical design, and delivery stages before generating code

multi_step_planning.methodology: Evaluation of plan-first workflow and plan adherence on multi-file tasks

evidence.value: Project guidelines files (.junie/guidelines.md) and IDE project model provide persistent project context; cross-session memory is thinner than dedicated knowledge-base systems

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of guidelines files, IDE index-backed context, and session persistence behavior

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Agentic debugging sets breakpoints and inspects program state to diagnose failures rather than guess-and-retry with print logging; iterates on failing tests

error_recovery.methodology: Assessment of debugger-driven failure diagnosis and test-iteration loops

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Single unified engine across IDE chat, tool window, and CLI via the Agent Communication Protocol (ACP); supports background/long-running tasks and CI-triggered runs, but no native multi-agent orchestration

agent_collaboration.methodology: Review of ACP-based surface unification, background task support, and absence of subagent/fan-out primitives

evidence.value: Runs in the developer's IDE/terminal with action approval modes (including a stricter approval mode versus 'brave' auto-run); no OS-level kernel-enforced sandbox documented

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Review of execution model: approval-gated local execution rather than kernel-level isolation; CI runs inherit the pipeline's isolation

evidence.value: Organization-level administration via JetBrains AI Enterprise and IDE Services: centralized provisioning, quota management, and model allowlisting for teams

access_control.methodology: Review of enterprise administration, license/quota controls, and per-user authentication (JetBrains Account, API keys, BYOK)

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Junie processes repository content and PR diffs (including via GitHub Actions/GitLab automation); injection-specific mitigations are not publicly documented

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Threat surface analysis of PR-review automation and repo content processing; no published injection defense documentation or third-party research found

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II report specifically covering Junie is available in the JetBrains Trust Center, alongside penetration testing reports and infrastructure security documentation

data_isolation.methodology: Review of Junie-scoped SOC 2 Type II attestation and JetBrains infrastructure security posture

evidence.value: Proprietary product under JetBrains AI Service Terms; public GitHub repo hosts installers, release management, and issue tracking, not agent source code

open_source_transparency.methodology: License and source availability review; open ACP protocol adoption partially offsets the closed core

evidence.value: JetBrains AI terms commit to not using customer code to train models; detailed collection settings and opt-outs are configurable per organization

data_retention.methodology: Review of JetBrains AI service terms, training commitments, and data collection settings

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II (Junie-scoped) and GDPR compliance documentation available; JetBrains is an EU-headquartered (Czech) vendor with long-standing enterprise compliance practice, including ISO 27001-aligned controls per its security knowledge base

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance certification and documentation review via the JetBrains Trust Center (NDA-gated reports)

evidence.value: Default operation routes code to third-party LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google); BYOK (including xAI, OpenRouter, GitHub Copilot) and local runtimes (Ollama, LM Studio, LiteLLM) let teams control the data path

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis of managed model routing versus BYOK/local alternatives

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Supports any model without lock-in: local runtimes via LiteLLM, LM Studio, and Ollama; JetBrains AI Enterprise offers on-premises deployment for organizations

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment covering local model runtimes and JetBrains AI Enterprise on-prem offering

evidence.value: Well-maintained documentation across the Junie site, JetBrains Help (licensing, quotas), and GA/CLI blog posts covering IDE, CLI, and CI usage

documentation_quality.methodology: Documentation completeness and accuracy review across product site, help center, and blogs

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Step-by-step visible actions in the IDE tool window with reviewable diffs; CLI and CI runs produce inspectable logs and PR review comments

execution_traceability.methodology: Review of in-IDE action visibility, diff review flow, and CI run artifacts

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Plan Mode documents requirements, technical design, and delivery stages before execution; PR reviews explain findings in project context

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of plan document quality and change justification in reviews

evidence.value: Closed-source agent; public repo (324 stars) is a distribution and issue-tracking channel only

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment of agent core and distribution artifacts

evidence.value: Active Discord community and GitHub issue tracking; backed by JetBrains' large IDE user base, though the standalone Junie community is younger and smaller than incumbent agents'

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis via Discord, GitHub activity, and JetBrains ecosystem reach

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: Ships inside all JetBrains IDEs with existing project models and run configurations; CLI installs via shell script, Homebrew, or npm; PR review via GitHub Actions and GitLab

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration surface assessment: zero-setup for the very large existing JetBrains IDE install base

evidence.value: CLI and CI/CD execution enable headless automation and PR-review pipelines; primarily a per-developer agent rather than a managed parallel cloud-agent fleet

scalability.methodology: Assessment of headless/CI usage and parallelism model; no managed cloud sandbox fleet comparable to cloud-agent products

evidence.value: Fixed subscription tiers: AI Free $0 (small monthly credit quota), AI Pro $10/mo, AI Ultimate $30/mo ($20/mo annual), AI Enterprise custom; credit top-ups at ~$1/credit; BYOK shifts inference cost to the customer's own provider

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis: predictable flat tiers with quota-based credits; complex agentic tasks can exhaust quotas quickly, and quota-to-task mapping is opaque

evidence.value: Per-user credit/quota usage tracking in the IDE and JetBrains Account; enterprise usage administration via JetBrains AI Enterprise, but no agent-specific observability stack (e.g., OTel export)

monitoring_capabilities.methodology: Review of quota dashboards and enterprise usage administration; limited dedicated agent telemetry

evidence.source: JetBrains blog - Junie out of beta
evidence.value: GA June 2026 on a unified ACP-based engine, backed by JetBrains' 25-year track record, millions of IDE users, and Junie-scoped SOC 2 Type II attestation

production_readiness.methodology: Maturity assessment combining GA status, vendor stability, and compliance attestation; agent itself has only weeks of GA history

code-generation.notes: Top-ranked on SWE-Rebench; strongest inside JetBrains IDEs where it leverages the debugger, inspections, and project model

data-analysis.notes: Good for code-centric data work (SQL via DataGrip connections, scripts); not an analytics product

research-assistant.notes: Codebase exploration and PR-context research are solid; general research is out of scope

education.notes: Plan documents and debugger-driven walkthroughs are instructive; free tier gives students a low-cost entry point

best_for: Teams already standardized on JetBrains IDEs wanting a native agent with zero new tooling
Developers who value debugger-driven fixing over log-and-retry agent loops
Organizations wanting model choice: managed models, BYOK, or fully local runtimes
PR review automation in GitHub Actions/GitLab with project-context awareness
Cost-sensitive teams preferring flat subscription tiers over usage-metered agents
strengths: Ranked #1 coding agent on independent SWE-Rebench (61.6% resolved, 72.7% pass@5)
Debugs with the IDE's real debugger: breakpoints, stack inspection, expression evaluation
Plan Mode produces structured requirements/design/delivery-stage documents before coding
LLM-agnostic: BYOK (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, OpenRouter) and local runtimes (Ollama, LM Studio, LiteLLM)
Runs everywhere JetBrains users work: IDE, terminal CLI, GitHub Actions, GitLab
Enterprise trust anchored in JetBrains: Junie-scoped SOC 2 Type II, Trust Center, and AI Enterprise on-prem option
Predictable flat pricing tiers, including a free tier
limitations: GA only since June 2026; limited production track record and third-party security research
Closed-source core; public GitHub repo is distribution-only
No OS-level sandbox for local execution; safety relies on approval modes
Prompt injection defenses are not publicly documented despite automated PR-review surface
Credit quotas are small (roughly 3/10/35 credits per month across Free/Pro/Ultimate) and complex agent tasks consume them quickly
No managed cloud agent fleet; parallel/background execution is bounded by local and CI resources
Strongest value requires the JetBrains IDE ecosystem; CLI-only users lose the debugger advantage

metadata.license: Proprietary (JetBrains AI Service Terms); public repo for distribution and issues
supported_models: BYOK: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, OpenRouter, GitHub Copilot
programming_languages: All languages supported by JetBrains IDEs (Java, Kotlin, Python, Go, JS/TS, PHP, C#, C++, Ruby, Rust, etc.)
metadata.deployment_type: IDE-embedded and local CLI; CI via GitHub Actions/GitLab; on-prem via JetBrains AI Enterprise
tool_support: PR review (GitHub Actions, GitLab, CLI, plugin)
metadata.first_release: Preview January 2025; CLI beta March 2026; GA June 2026
metadata.pricing: Included in JetBrains AI tiers: AI Free $0, AI Pro $10/mo, AI Ultimate $30/mo ($20/mo annual), AI Enterprise custom; credit-quota based with ~$1/credit top-ups; BYOK available
